name: Osan Air Base
tagline: The most forward-deployed permanently based wing in the Air Force: 7th Air Force and the 51st Fighter Wing, near Pyeongtaek.
_note: OCONUS (South Korea). US-only feeds omitted (overseas uses OHA, not BAH; no RentCast or SchoolDigger). Weather via Open-Meteo fallback. Facts verified from official sources (osan.af.mil, dodea.edu/pacific, TRICARE Overseas, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Command sponsorship is central to a Korea tour. Near Camp Humphreys (Army). Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx).

data.Location: Songtan, Pyeongtaek, South Korea (40 mi south of Seoul)
data.Distinction: The most forward-deployed permanently based wing in the Air Force

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Osan Air Base is home to Headquarters Seventh Air Force and the 51st Fighter Wing, which flies the A-10 Thunderbolt II and F-16 Fighting Falcon. It is the most forward-deployed permanently based wing in the Air Force.
It sits in Songtan, part of Pyeongtaek, about 40 miles south of Seoul and not far from the Army's Camp Humphreys, anchoring U.S. airpower on the Korean Peninsula.

sections.title: Command Sponsorship and a Korea Tour

data.subtitle: As at any Korea base, the first question is whether your tour is accompanied (command-sponsored). It decides whether your family comes and whether you get housing, schools, and full benefits, and OHA covers off-base rent.
intro: Korea assignments come in accompanied and unaccompanied versions. Command sponsorship brings your family at government expense and unlocks on-base housing, DoDEA schools, and the commissary. Confirm it early through your gaining unit before you make plans.

If you're command-sponsored, you'll choose between on-base housing and renting off base in Songtan and the wider Pyeongtaek area, where OHA covers rent up to a ceiling. The area is well used to the American community.

recommendations.advice: Nothing else matters until command sponsorship is approved; work it through your unit early.
On-base housing keeps you close to work, schools, and amenities; demand is high, so get on the list early.
Songtan and the Pyeongtaek area offer apartments on the local economy; OHA offsets rent and utilities up to a ceiling.
Seoul, fast trains, and budget flights make Korea a launchpad for travel across Asia, and Camp Humphreys' amenities are close by.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Access and in-processing are handled through the installation, and overseas access rules differ from stateside bases. You will need status documents under the U.S.-Korea SOFA.
Confirm the current visitor and access requirements on the official Osan AB site before you arrive.

sections.title: Housing & OHA
data: Command-sponsored families choose between on-base government housing and renting off base in Songtan and the surrounding Pyeongtaek area. On-base housing is convenient and in demand.

Overseas, the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA) replaces stateside BAH for those living off base, individualized by rank, dependents, and actual rent up to a ceiling. The Housing Office assigns on-base housing and helps with off-base rentals.

sections.title: Schools
data: Children attend Department of Defense Education Activity (DoDEA) schools rather than local districts. DoDEA Pacific operates schools serving the Osan community (Osan American Elementary and Middle/High School).
Start DoDEA registration early and confirm boundaries and required records on the DoDEA site as you plan your move.

data.Care: Outpatient primary and specialty care; inpatient care referred to Brian D. Allgood Army Community Hospital at Camp Humphreys or host-nation facilities

intro: An overseas PCS has extra steps beyond a stateside move. Start these early, ideally before you leave.

steps.title: Confirm command sponsorship
steps.text: An accompanied (command-sponsored) tour is what lets your family come at government expense and access housing, schools, and the commissary. Confirm it through your gaining unit before you plan anything.

steps.title: Get no-fee passports and SOFA documents
steps.text: Apply for no-fee passports for family members and the U.S.-Korea SOFA documents that let you live in-country. Tourist passports are separate.

steps.title: Line up lodging and a sponsor
steps.text: Arrange on-base temporary lodging and connect with your unit sponsor before you fly. You can claim Temporary Lodging Allowance (TLA).

steps.title: Plan household goods and your vehicle
steps.text: Overseas shipments go by sea with weight limits, and there are rules on shipping a vehicle. Confirm what you can bring and the timelines.

steps.title: Register the kids with DoDEA
steps.text: Children attend DoDEA Pacific schools; start registration early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up TRICARE Overseas
steps.text: Enroll in TRICARE Overseas and get a primary care manager at the 51st Medical Group clinic.

data.a: Headquarters Seventh Air Force and the 51st Fighter Wing (A-10s and F-16s), the most forward-deployed permanently based wing in the Air Force.

It's official approval for your family to accompany you at government expense. It unlocks on-base housing, DoDEA schools, the commissary, and benefits. Without it, a Korea tour is typically unaccompanied. Confirm it early.
On-base housing is convenient and in demand; many also rent off base in Songtan and the Pyeongtaek area with OHA. The Army's Camp Humphreys and its amenities are close by.
No. Overseas you receive OHA (for off-base rentals) instead of BAH, individualized by rank, dependents, and actual rent up to a ceiling. On-base housing is government-provided.
